prerequisites: A Facebook Page that you administer, plus its numeric Page ID (set as page_id).
A Meta (Facebook) developer app created at developers.facebook.com to authenticate Graph API requests.
An admin or equivalent role on the target Page so you can generate a Page access token for it.
A User access token granted the permissions needed to list and read the Page (for example pages_show_list and pages_read_engagement), used to mint the Page access token.

authMethods.name: Page access token
authMethods.summary: The access_token secret field expects a Facebook Page access token. Obtain it by calling GET /{user-id}/accounts (or /me/accounts) on the Graph API with a User access token that holds the required Page permissions; the response returns each managed Page's ID and its Page access token. The connector sends this token as the access_token parameter to authorize Graph API requests against your Page. Tokens are short-lived (1-2 hours) by default; exchange for a long-lived token (about 60 days) for unattended syncs.

setupSteps.title: Create a Meta developer app
setupSteps.body: Sign in at developers.facebook.com and create an app. The app is what authenticates your Graph API calls; Page access tokens are unique to each Page, admin, and app combination.

setupSteps.title: Grant Page permissions and get a User access token
setupSteps.body: Using your app, obtain a User access token (for example via the Graph API Explorer or Facebook Login) with the permissions required to list and read your Page, such as pages_show_list and pages_read_engagement. You must hold an admin or equivalent role on the Page.

setupSteps.title: Generate the Page access token
setupSteps.body: Call GET https://graph.facebook.com/{your-user-id}/accounts?access_token={user-access-token}. The response lists Pages you manage along with each Page's ID and Page access token. Copy the Page access token for your target Page into the connector's access_token field. Exchange the short-lived token for a long-lived one if you need it to survive beyond a couple of hours.

setupSteps.title: Set the Page ID
setupSteps.body: Find the numeric ID of your Page (returned in the /accounts response above, or listed in the Page's About section) and enter it in the page_id field. This is the Page the connector reads from.

setupSteps.title: Tune page size (optional)
setupSteps.body: Optionally set page_size to control how many records are requested per Graph API page when fetching Page data. Leave it unset to use the default.

sources.title: Facebook Pages API - Get Started (Meta for Developers)
Access Tokens for Meta Technologies (Facebook Login guide)
Page - Graph API Reference (Meta for Developers)
